summary: Large Kirby copy-ability decomp/matching PR that split the former monolithic ftKb_Init.c Kirby range into multiple ftKb_SpecialN* translation units and added thousands of lines of partial/matching C for Kirby neutral-special/copy-ability behavior. It updated GALE01 splits and configure.py to build the new objects, added/refined Kirby types and item headers, and made small call-site/type fixes in CaptureKirby, items, and result-player code. Review centered on accepting incremental LLM-assisted partial matches while cleaning obvious slop: prefer GET_FIGHTER over raw gobj->user_data, use motion-state and FighterKind enums instead of raw integers, avoid unnecessary casts by fixing variable/field types, and rename generic var_* loop temporaries to i/j/result. The final discussion explicitly accepted a small match-percent regression from GET_FIGHTER cleanup as worthwhile because matched locked-in functions still diffed cleanly.
what_changed: Split the Kirby object range in config/GALE01/splits.txt: ftKb_Init.c now ends at 0x800F1FDC and new objects cover ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, ftKb_SpecialNFx.c, ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ftKb_SpecialNZd.c, and ftKb_SpecialNYs.c through 0x8010D740, with corresponding sdata2 boundaries.
Added the new Kirby split files to configure.py as NonMatching objects so the build links the newly separated translation units.

Added large amounts of C for Kirby neutral special/copy abilities and related actions, including functions around ftKb_SpecialN_800F190C, ftKb_SpecialN_800F1D24, ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C, fn_800F21E8, Kirby AttackDash wrappers, SpecialHi animation/IASA handlers, and many copy-ability-specific files.
Replaced many raw motion-state constants with ftKb_MS_* names, e.g. ftKb_MS_AttackDash and ftKb_MS_AttackDashAir in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls.

Replaced direct gobj->user_data access with GET_FIGHTER(gobj) broadly across Kirby files; author reported 354 replacements and zero remaining gobj->user_data in the ftKirby files after the cleanup, while keeping PAD_STACK where required for stack-frame matching.

Updated ftCo_CaptureKirby.c to pass a Vec3 output parameter to ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B5C and use the returned victim_pos when updating capture Kirby positioning, rather than ignoring the target position.

Added/refined includes and declarations for fighter common modules, Kirby headers, Link types, item headers, camera/effects/collision/library headers, and function forward declarations needed by the newly materialized Kirby code.

Touched Kirby/item headers such as types.h, itkirby_2F23.h, itkirbycutterbeam.h, and itseakneedleheld.h to support the new call signatures/types; exact header semantics are only weakly visible in the excerpt.
Made smaller cleanup/fix changes in itbox.c and gmresultplayer.c, but the provided excerpt does not show their details.
smart_moves: Split the large Kirby range by object/float boundaries instead of leaving all new code in ftKb_Init.c; maintainers confirmed the posted splitting rationale was consistent with hand/software splitter practice.
Centralized partial Kirby copy-ability progress into master despite not all functions being perfect, enabling future contributors to improve on a shared baseline instead of duplicating work.
Used build-system and split-file updates together: config/GALE01/splits.txt and configure.py were changed in tandem so newly split translation units had matching address ranges and build objects.

Accepted a small global match-percent drop from GET_FIGHTER cleanup because ninja diff still passed and no locked-in matched functions were broken, prioritizing maintainable decomp style over raw partial-match percentage.
Preserved PAD_STACK where needed after GET_FIGHTER substitutions, acknowledging that the inline macro can change stack-frame rounding and sometimes needs explicit padding for matching.
Converted raw enum-like values to named enums: motion states to ftKb_MS_* and fighter-kind comparisons to FTKIND_* names, improving readability without intended functional change.
Posted LLM/file-splitting reasoning transparently and asked maintainers to validate it, which surfaced project policy and style guidance for future LLM-assisted mass matching.
decomp_lessons: For LLM-assisted mass matching, matching percentage alone is not a sufficient quality metric; review should still remove unnecessary casts, fake-looking temporaries, raw numeric enums, and unreadable m2c-style artifacts.

Project tolerance in this discussion favored incremental progress, but maintainers debated thresholds: ribbanya argued to submit anything that compiles and improves match percent, while PsiLupan suggested avoiding very low-quality m2c-like output and mentioned roughly 90% as personal preference with 85% reasonable for some smaller/LLM mass matches.

When a cast appears solely to force matching, first check whether the local or struct field should have the correct type instead; review example: replace a suspicious `(s32) fp->fv.kb.x60` pattern by making `x60` an int/s32-compatible value.
Prefer project access macros such as GET_FIGHTER(gobj) over raw `gobj->user_data`, even if the macro changes stack layout; add or tune PAD_STACK only where diffing proves it is needed.
GET_FIGHTER can eliminate some padding, but not always; ideal workflow is to compare each affected function with and without PAD_STACK after macro substitution.
Named enums should replace raw hex motion-state IDs and raw fighter-kind literals as soon as the meaning is known; this makes mass-matched code easier to audit and maintain.
For file splitting, respect text and sdata2/float boundaries in splits.txt and verify the resulting objects link properly.
Small semantic signature fixes can emerge from matching work; in this PR, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B5C was changed to fill a Vec3 output used by ftCo_CaptureKirby positioning.
naming_conventions: Use `ftKb_MS_*` motion-state enum names instead of raw values like `0x15F`, `0x160`, `0x179`, or `0x19D` when calling Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

Use `FTKIND_*` names instead of numeric fighter-kind literals, e.g. `FTKIND_KIRBY`, `FTKIND_MARIO`, `FTKIND_KOOPA`, `FTKIND_LINK`, `FTKIND_NANA`, `FTKIND_LUIGI`, `FTKIND_MARS`, `FTKIND_ZELDA`, `FTKIND_FALCO`, `FTKIND_GANON`, `FTKIND_PURIN`.
Avoid generic `var_r4`, `var_r5`, `var_r0`, and `new_var` names when the role is clear; loop indices should usually be `i` and `j`, and result temporaries can be named `result`.
Name positional temporaries by role, e.g. the CaptureKirby update introduced `victim_pos` for the Vec3 returned by ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B5C.

For simple directional offsets, prefer a direct expression or clearly named variable over an opaque temp; review suggested `pos.x += fp->facing_dir == 1.0f ? da->specialhi_projectile_spawn_x : -da->specialhi_projectile_spawn_x`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` for Fighter access, but be aware its inline expansion can round the stack frame to the nearest 8 bytes; add `PAD_STACK` selectively when needed to preserve matching.
Do not blanket-remove PAD_STACK after macro cleanup; compare diff output per function because some functions still require padding after GET_FIGHTER substitution.

Permuting high-percent partial matches may not improve if the underlying issue is incorrect types, wrong field modeling, or code-shape assumptions rather than statement order; the author reported overnight permuter runs on >99% functions with no improvements.
Changing a variable or struct field type is often cleaner than adding an explicit cast to coerce codegen.
Splitting large translation units should be validated against float/sdata2 boundaries and link behavior, not just source organization.

When style cleanup slightly reduces aggregate partial-match percent, use the authoritative diff/build result for locked-in matched functions; in this PR, `ninja diff passes` was used to justify accepting the GET_FIGHTER cleanup.
review_feedback: ribbanya told the author force-pushing was unnecessary because the PR would be squashed.
ribbanya encouraged submitting compiling partial progress instead of dropping anything below 95%, emphasizing centralizing decomp progress on master.
PsiLupan cautioned that a cutoff may be needed because ~60% functions can be practically raw m2c output; he personally tries to avoid below 90%, with 85% possibly reasonable for smaller functions.
ribbanya later called 85% a reasonable threshold for this kind of LLM mass matching, while still valuing incremental progress.
PsiLupan flagged suspicious casts such as casting `fp->fv.kb.x60` to s32 and asked why the variable was not simply s32/int.
ribbanya noted Claude/LLM output tends to generate unnecessary casts and suggested fixing the type of `x60` instead.
Reviewers asked to rename obvious indices instead of leaving `var` names; examples included using `i` and `j` for standard iterations.
ribbanya requested teaching the process to use `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` because it can often make padding unnecessary.
ribbanya clarified that if padding is still required, use `GET_FIGHTER` plus `PAD_STACK`, because the macro's inline function call affects stack-frame rounding.
PsiLupan suggested simplifying a facing-direction projectile spawn offset with a ternary expression to remove a temp and improve naming/readability.
PsiLupan confirmed the posted file-splitting summary was accurate relative to prior hand and software splitting practices.

Author reported applying feedback: motion-state enums, FTKIND enums, variable renames, GET_FIGHTER, newline fixes, field type fix, and clang-format; he also noted a GET_FIGHTER/PAD_STACK blanket replacement caused a small partial-match regression.
follow_up_queries: Inspect the final merged diff for src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/types.h to capture the exact struct/field type fixes introduced for Kirby copy ability state.
Search later PRs for ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B5C and CaptureKirby to see whether the Vec3 output signature was renamed or further typed.
Search later ftKirby PRs for cleanup of remaining `new_var`, `var_f1`, raw masks, and placeholder includes from this mass-matching pass.
Compare the final merged ftKb_SpecialN* files against this PR's discussion to identify functions where GET_FIGHTER plus PAD_STACK was needed and document those matching cases.
Look for follow-up PRs by malvarezcastillo on Kirby copy abilities after PR #2138, especially promised smaller PRs with dropped functions or additional matches.
Audit item header changes for itkirby_2F23.h, itkirbycutterbeam.h, and itseakneedleheld.h to determine which function prototypes were changed to support Kirby copy abilities.
